title: Celebrity stagnation — 420K stars, 1 commit/90 days
description: 419K stars + 45K forks but 1 commit in 90 days. 1,197 open issues. Social signal is top-20 GitHub, throughput is near zero.
recommendation: Fork or monitor. Don't treat as active reference.

title: PR approval bottleneck — 14/15 runs action_required
description: CI workflows require maintainer approval to run on fork PRs. Approvals aren't happening. Community contributions can't land even when automation would pass.

title: OpenSSF Scorecard 2.3/10
description: Zero scores for Branch-Protection, SAST, Security-Policy, Fuzzing, CII-Best-Practices. Only License (10) and Binary-Artifacts (10) are high. Low blast radius (curated list, not runtime code).

title: Commercial capture — APILayer sponsorship before community content
description: README opens with APILayer banner + 10 promoted APIs before the community index. Community labor channeled into commercial lead-gen surface.
recommendation: Cautionary pattern for any community distribution model.

title: Bus factor 2
description: Top 2 contributors (matheusfelipeog 826 + davemachado 683) = 1,509 commits vs next 8 all ≤150. Neither actively merging.

title: POSITIVE: Link validation workflow — transferable pattern
description: validate_links.yml + scripts/validate/links.py (273 lines) + tests (172 lines). Scheduled + on-push URL liveness checking. Directly transferable for docs/*, skill-indexes, README.
recommendation: Extract pattern for sonash documentation link checking.

title: POSITIVE: Format validation scripts with tests
description: format.py (277 lines) + tests (466 lines). Enforces table format, category structure, alphabetical order, description length, auth/HTTPS/CORS enum values. Simpler version of patterns:check.

title: Massive absolute engagement — top-20 GitHub repo
description: 419K stars, 45K forks, 4,572 watchers, 1,197 open issues. Valuable as a reference for 'what peak awesome-list looks like' — and what happens after peak.
